text_blocks.snippet: Reunification counselling services in Vancouver and the Lower Mainland, BC, are primarily designed to help families, children, and youth reconnect following separation, high-conflict divorce, estrangement, or involvement with child protection services. Services are available through both ministry-referred programs (often free) and private specialized practices.
These services are generally free but require a referral from the Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD).

list.snippet: Family Services of Greater Vancouver (FSGV): Offers Family Preservation and Reunification Services to help families increase safety and well-being. Services include in-home counseling, parenting education, and therapeutic supervised access.
Touchstone Family Association: Provides Family Preservation & Family Reunification services, including in-home/office counselling for families in crisis.
Hollyburn Community Services Society: Offers intensive outreach counselling for families with children under 19, focusing on child protection concerns.
Vancouver Aboriginal Child and Family Services Society (VACFSS): Provides specialized, culturally sensitive support for Indigenous families. Hollyburn Family Services +4

reConnect Families (Vancouver/Surrey/Fraser Valley): A team of therapists, counsellors, and divorce coaches focusing on parent-child reunification in high-conflict or alienated families. They offer in-home, office, and weekend intensive sessions.
FAMILY FORWARD (Alyson Jones & Associates): Specialized in reunification therapy and counselling.
Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody): Offers a reunification program focused on play-based sessions to rebuild relationships after separation or divorce.
Leanne Toews (Surrey/Online): Provides a 6-step structured, partly virtual family reunification program. Bright Star Counselling +4
High Conflict/Alienation: Addressing cases where children resist contact with a parent due to estrangement.
Supervised Access: Offering therapeutic, supervised visits to support safe reconnection.
Parent Education: Teaching skills to manage child-parent conflicts and improve communication.
Child Support: Helping children process emotions related to separation. HelpStartsHere +3

text_blocks.snippet: For ministry-funded programs, you must speak with your MCFD Social Worker. For private services, you can contact the agencies directly, such as reConnect Families at (604) 780-7945 or Bright Star Counselling. Bright Star Counselling +2
Note: For immediate crisis support, you can call 9-8-8 (Suicide Crisis Helpline) or 310-6789 (Mental Health Support) in BC. HelpStartsHere
